name: Proto-Tamil Hypothesis (Ponmuthu Shanmugham)
affiliation: Independent; Member of Technical Staff (Retd), Lucent Technologies – Bell Laboratories
contact: vpshanmugham@yahoo.com; 8112 Northway Drive, Hanover Park, IL 60133
publications: Ancient Harappans Speak! After 5000 years (ISBN 979-8-9949362-3-5 pb; 979-8-9940362-4-2 eBook); Indus Script is a Language, A living language of 80 million (ISBN 979-8-9940362-6-6, Kavin Publishers 2026); Reading Indus-Harappan Script: Research Keys (ISBN 979-8-9940362-9-7, 2026)
extends: Mahadevan (1977) concordance; Parpola (1994) Dravidian framework; Purnachandra Jeeva (2020) ISBN 978-81-9427-9105
key_claims: 10 vowels + 18 consonants mapped to Tamil; tally marks as phonetic placeholders for Tamil vowel u; motifs as urban street identifiers; 400+ seals read (potentially 2400); seal brevity explained by purpose not limitation
future_validation: GC-MS spectroscopy on shards near ghee seals; stratigraphic correlation; Tamil family comparative study

name: Dravidian Hypothesis (Iravatham Mahadevan)
affiliation: Honorary Consultant, Indus Research Centre, Roja Muthiah Research Library (RMRL), Chennai
publications: The Indus Script: Texts, Concordance and Tables. Archaeological Survey of India Memoir No. 77, 1977
key_claims: Standard sign concordance (Mahadevan numbering); foundational corpus work; most frequent sign is the jar/cow sign

name: Proto-Dravidian Hypothesis (Asko Parpola)
publications: Deciphering the Indus Script. Cambridge University Press, 1994. Also: Corpus of Indus Seals and Inscriptions Vol.1 (with J.P. Joshi, Helsinki 1987), Vol.2 (with S.G.G.M. Shah, Helsinki 1991)
key_claims: Rebus principle mapping; Proto-Dravidian phonetics; most widely accepted academic framework

name: Non-Linguistic Emblem Thesis (Farmer, Sproat, Witzel)
researcher: Steve Farmer (U Penn); Richard Sproat (U of Illinois); Michael Witzel (Harvard University)
affiliation: University of Pennsylvania; University of Illinois; Harvard University
language_claimed: None — signs are not speech encoding
publications: The Collapse of the Indus Script Thesis: The myth of a literate Harappan civilization. Electronic Journal of Vedic Studies, 02/11/2004
key_claims: High singleton rate; brevity; lack of complex syntax; no bilingual text; signs are emblems not speech

name: Statistical Language-Structure Hypothesis (Rao et al.)
researcher: Rajesh P.N. Rao; Nisha Yadav; et al.
affiliation: University of Washington; Deccan College Pune
language_claimed: Unknown — language-like structure demonstrated, not decoded
publications: Entropic Evidence for Linguistic Structure in the Indus Script. Science 324:1165, 2009. DOI: 10.1126/science.1170391. Also: N. Yadav et al. sign frequency distribution (Harappa.com)
key_claims: Conditional entropy of Indus signs consistent with natural languages; inconsistent with random or simple non-linguistic systems

name: Proto-Tamil Hypothesis (Purnachandra Jeeva)
publications: sinthuveLiyil munthu thamiz (சிந்துவெளியில் முந்து தமிழ்). Yalisaip Pathippagam, 2020. ISBN 978-81-9427-9105 (Tamil language)
key_claims: Tamil syllabic structure of Indus script identified; many seals read in Tamil. VPS2024 extends and modifies Jeeva's phonemes and morphemes.
